sanskrit: यया तु धर्मकामार्थान्धृत्या धारयते जुहुन। प्रसङ्गेन फलाकाङ्क्षी धृतिः सा पार्थ राजसी॥
transliteration: yayā tu dharma-kāmārthān dhṛtyā dhārayate ’rjuna | prasaṅgena phalākāṅkṣī dhṛtiḥ sā pārtha rājasī ||
literal_meaning: That by which one sustains religious duty, desire, and wealth—O Arjuna—through clinging, fruit-desiring; that determination, O Pārtha, is of the mode of passion.
contextual_meaning: Determination becomes rajasic when it is tethered to outcome: the mind uses will-power not for inner alignment but to secure external rewards—merit, money, pleasure. The energy is strong but cyclic, feeding anxiety and burnout because every gain must be defended and every loss avenged.
psychological_state: High-functioning burnout: the person looks disciplined but is internally driven by a hidden contract—‘I must produce to be loved.’ Will-power is colonized by the ego, producing chronic hyper-arousal and a compulsive scoreboard mentality.
trigger_scenario: A manager meditates at 5 a.m. to “crush the day,” posts the achievement on social media, then checks stock prices before breakfast—equating spiritual practice with productivity points.
anchor_text: Work with steady will, not for trophies, but for the rightness of the work itself.
